items.title: 2 Top Dividend Stocks I'd Own Over the Next Decade
items.description: While Wall Street obsesses over the next hot AI stock or crypto play, two blue-chip dividend stalwarts quietly keep printing money for shareholders: Visa (V) and The Coca-Cola Company (KO). These aren't flashy picks. But over the next decade, they could prove far more valuable than chasing the ...

items.title: 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy in 2026 and Hold Forever
items.description: All three offer much more than a regular payout.

items.title: Bill Ackman Questions Credit Card Rewards Structure, Says Low-Income Consumers Subsidize Premium Cardholders Amid Trump Rate Cap Debate
items.description: Bill Ackman criticized credit card rewards programs, saying low-income consumers end up subsidizing benefits for wealthy cardholders.

items.title: Is American Express Stock a Millionaire Maker?
items.author: Neil Patel
items.description: American Express has been a historically successful investment with 4,570% total returns over 30 years, driven by strong competitive advantages including network effects and premium brand positioning. However, the article cautions that current valuations are steep at a P/E ratio of 25.7, significantly above the 10-year average, making it an unattractive buying opportunity despite solid long-term growth prospects of 10%+ revenue and mid-teens EPS growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company demonstrates strong competitive advantages, durable business model, and solid growth prospects (10%+ revenue, mid-teens EPS growth), the current valuation at P/E 25.7 is significantly elevated above historical averages, making it not a smart buying opportunity at present prices despite being a high-quality business.
